nodes.text:
You stop walking and realize you've managed to get turned around in a part of town you thought you knew well.

What you do know is that were walking somewhere specific but details have slipped from your mind. You were meeting someone, or going home, or going... someplace. It doesn't matter now. You turned left instead of right and now you are walking down an alley you have never seen in a part of town you thought you knew.

The chill in the air has a real bite to it. It sits deep in the concrete. It comes up through your shoes and you can feel it in your knees. Your breath is visible and the sodium street lights turn everything the color of old pennies.

As if waking from a dream, you suddenly notice the Sound. You feel it before you hear it. It's not music - more like pressure. Low-frequency, in the hinge of your jaw and the flat of your sternum. It has no melody, but it does have direction. You tell yourself it was your decision to walk towards the source, but maybe you were already doing that without realizing it.

conditional_text.append: Other people are walking the same direction. Not together — parallel. A woman in a green coat moving quickly. A guy with his head tilted, tracking the same pressure as you. Several people have a curious look on their face and you get the impression that, like you, this wasn't their original destination.

choices.text: Follow the bass. Walk down the alley.

nodes.subtitle: THE BEGINNING

nodes.text:
The alley opens onto a gravel lot. Orange light bleeds from the seams of a building at the far end — a warehouse, something with the bones of industrial purpose. The bass is coming from inside. It is not necessarily *louder* here. It is closer, denser. You notice the distinction without understanding it.

The door on the front of the building is open. Warm air pushes through the gap — sweat and fog-machine glycerin and something underneath both, metallic, almost mineral, like the air before a thunderstorm but indoors.

There's a security guard standing by the door talking to the woman in the green coat you saw earlier. You hear the woman say "No I don't have anything in my left pocket" before she disappears into the building.

arrival_variants.text: You've arrived at the source of the Sound. When did you start seeking it? Have you been following this Sound all night? Have you heard this Sound before?
You've arrived at the source of the Sound.
You've arrived at the source of the Sound. When did you start seeking it? Have you been following this Sound for your entire life? You can't help but feel like you've been here before.

conditional_text.append: You notice a group of people standing by the loading dock on the side of the building. The door is propped open and they're about to head back inside.

choices.text: Talk to the security guard and try to get in.
Walk over to the group by the loading dock.

nodes.subtitle: THE WAREHOUSE

nodes.text:
The room is wrong in a way you can't immediately name.

The ceiling is too high — higher than the building looked from outside, disappearing into dark above the fog and the moving lights. The floor is concrete, cracked in places, and a projection covers most of it: concentric rings pulsing slowly, expanding and contracting like something breathing. You step around the rings without thinking about it. Everyone does. The rings feel like they belong to something.

The crowd is maybe a hundred people. Dancing, some of them. The rest stand in loose clusters near the walls, holding drinks, talking in fragments between bass hits. It's a party. You've been to parties.

The dance floor is melding but still emerging. A girl pushes her hair back from her face and two people near her raise a hand to their heads. You're not sure. The light is doing something — strobing low and orange — and in the gaps between pulses the bodies rearrange and you can't tell if they moved the same way or if the dark just took a frame out. You watch longer. You stop being sure what you're watching for.

The air is warm and thick and tastes like ozone. You breathe through your mouth and that's worse.

arrival_variants.text: You're inside. The Sound has structure now. The heat is thick and close.

conditional_text.append: A woman near you is swaying with her eyes closed. Scuffed white sneakers, a pen mark on the left toe — a tiny star. She looks like she forgot anyone could see her.

choices.text: Move into the dance floor, towards the speaker stack

nodes.subtitle: NON-EUCLIDEAN BEATS

nodes.text:
The DJ decks are at the far wall. The fog is thickest there — shapes in the murk, orange and yellow, something bright. Two hands move across the mixer. Pink gloves. The hands are precise and mechanical, adjusting knobs with the efficiency of someone threading a machine, not performing.

Above the hands, something large tilts. An ear. The size of a satellite dish, oriented toward the crowd like radar.

You look away. You look back. The fog has shifted. Eyes — green, clustered, not all pointing the same direction. One of them was already pointed at you before you looked.

The entity behind the turntables is not just playing music. It is operating a machine. A machine that appears to both feed on and manipulate the people and energy of the dance floor in some kind of insane feedback loop. Meanwhile, the bass is emanating from a direction your inner ear cannot understand.

arrival_variants.text: You are close enough to see the gloves flex at the knuckle joints. The motion is peristaltic, involuntary. You are not sure what you're seeing but you do know that you're feeling a mix of unsettled horror and curiosity.

conditional_text.append: Your instinct was right. The wrongness was not the place itself. It was the purpose that this place is being put to.

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR adjusts the low end. Three people near the front stop talking mid-sentence and begin to move. They do not know they were corrected.
nodes.subtitle: DJ OOR

nodes.text:
The crowd is filling in around you. You can feel it like an electrical field — warmth with weight and direction, like a swelling river that wants a stone to carry. The floor pulls on you. The rings pulse and oscillate. The mechanism is patient but unyielding.

The door is still behind you. You can feel that too — cold at the back of your neck, the alley, the walk home, the sodium lights. A life where tonight was just a night you went somewhere and came back and nothing was different in the morning.

arrival_variants.text: Something is about to happen. The collective energy around you is aligning and you can feel your identity of self starting to soften.

conditional_text.append: The thing that pulled you here is still pulling. It has not stopped since you first sensed it. The more you come to understand, the stronger the pull. Is the machine getting stronger or are you getting more attuned?

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR does not look at you. The eye that tracked you earlier was not looking — it was measuring.

choices.text: Release yourself into the crowd. Submit to the dance.
The door. The cold. The walk home.

nodes.subtitle: THE DECISION

nodes.text:
You meld in with the crowd as the energy of the dance floor begins to flow. Reflections of familiarity and anchored memory flood into your mind. You don't know the name of this song, but you recognize its signature.

Call-and-response - a structure you've felt a thousand times: in church basements and block parties, at weddings and school dances. To the left, to the left, to the left, to the left. To the right, to the right, to the right, to the right. A classic dance floor warmup - but the vocals are wrong - vocoded, arriving from underneath or behind or inside. Your body doesn't care. Your body hears the call.

You are moving. You didn't decide to move. You submitted to a nostalgic dance with a beat loud enough, people dense enough, and the fog disorienting enough that self-consciousness gets muscled out by something older and less interested in your opinion.

The crowd is synchronized. Felt, not seen. Two hundred bodies at the same time. The floor vibrates harder. The temperature climbs.

The person next to you - tall, beard, house paint on his knuckles from trim work - is laughing. You make eye contact and something passes between you that is deeper than language. You are both part of the same movement, the same energy pattern, the same moment in time and space, right here right now. You both understand this. DJ OOR understands this.

The song ends. Synchronization loosens but doesn't fully release. Strangers standing closer now without having negotiated the distance. The meta-patterns are aligning, the system harmonics are self-feeding and beginning to resonate.

arrival_variants.text: You submit to the attunement of the dance floor. The crowd accepts you.

conditional_text.append: You are laughing. This surprises you. Nothing about this should be funny and yet you can't help yourself.

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR makes an adjustment below 40Hz. The projection rings accelerate. The room contracts and no one notices.

choices.text: That was intense - let's get a drink.

nodes.subtitle: THE OOR SLIDE

nodes.text:
You get a drink. Not for the hydration - for the excuse to stand still. The bar is along the far and when you step off the floor the temperature drops. Not gradually, but like a border. You hold your cup with both hands.

From here you can see the whole room. The dance floor is a single organism, breathing and contracting.

The sneaker-star woman is still out there — eyes closed, swaying. She is wearing a smile.


A woman walks past you toward the floor. She's wearing antlers - not plastic, not symmetrical. Organic. Grown? She is carrying a thin dark rod and as she passes the rod chirps.

The woman stops. She doesn't look at you. She looks at the rod. She measures the air around you, carefully manipulating the rod into the corners and pockets of the space between you and her. She pauses and moves on. You have been measured.

The cold is starting to bother you. Not physically - existentially. The heart of the system pulls at you. Patiently. The door feels farther than when you came in but you weren't thinking about leaving anyway, right?

arrival_variants.text: The cold finds you the moment you step off the floor. Not gradually — a border.
The bar is along the wall. The cold side.

conditional_text.append: From here DJ OOR is more visible. The texture is fibrous — fur or crochet or something without a textile name. The ear rotates slowly, scanning.

nodes.subtitle: AT THE BAR

nodes.text:
The music changes. The texture on top of the bass is different now - melodic lines that curl and retreat, a timbre that doesn't map to any instrument you know. Whimsical in the way that fairy tales are whimsical, which is to say it is beautiful and it is not safe.

Someone is near you - closer than a stranger should be, and you don't mind. The bass is inside your skeleton now. The boundary between your body and the physical space itself has become blurry. The crowd is dancing with itself; the collective structures are energized.

arrival_variants.text: The music continues and the energy flows.

conditional_text.append: You're dancing. You haven't dance like this since, well, you're not really sure. Have you ever danced like this?
The paint-knuckle man is near you again. He grabs your shoulder and yells something you can't hear. It doesn't matter what he said. The gesture was the content.

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR's ear sweeps the room once. Density is sufficient. For the first time tonight, the construct makes no adjustment. The room is doing the work on its own.
nodes.subtitle: MOVEMENT IN UNITY

nodes.text:
The antlered woman has moved into the dance floor. A second antlered companion has appeared - moving differently, more structured, choreographic. Together they are a singular unit. The first holds the rod and it is resonating now - not humming but a sound that sits between the notes of the music, filling the spaces the melody leaves empty.

You watch her approach the sneaker-star woman. The rod sings. She takes her hand and guides her toward a dancer standing with their eyes half-closed, moving with their whole body, unselfconscious. The antlered woman positions them like someone tuning an instrument, adjusting the distance until the rod goes quiet. When it goes quiet, they look at each other and begin to move together without discussion.

This is happening everywhere. Pairs forming across the floor. Specific and deliberate. The room is breaking apart and reforming. It looks like the party getting looser. It looks like people finding their groove.

arrival_variants.text: You're not sure what you're seeing, but you understand it has a deeper, almost systemic purpose.

conditional_text.append: Some people are passed over. The rod doesn't respond to them. They don't notice being passed over. You wonder what it means to not be noticed by the rod. You wonder what it means that it chirped at you.

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR does not watch the familiars work. DJ OOR watches what the familiars produce.

choices.text: The warmth pulls you back to the floor.
This is getting weird. You've seen enough.

nodes.subtitle: THE MATING RITUAL

nodes.text:
You feel the shift immediately in the floor below you. The concentric rings surrounding the dance floor contract to a single circle of light, maybe eight feet across. Through the floor you see flashes of alien textures that, if you were to think too hard about, would drive your logical mind mad. 


Inside the circle, an arrow appears. As it starts to spin, the crowd pulls back instinctively to form a ring of bodies around the ring of light. The room holds its breath.

The arrow points at a woman near the far side - jacket still on despite the heat. She looks at the arrow. The crowd looks at her. The weight of two hundred pairs of eyes should be crushing. Instead, something in her shifts. She steps in. She dances - not well, not badly. Honestly. Thirty seconds of being completely visible and not caring. The crowd erupts and the machine upshifts into a higher energy state.

The paint-knuckle man enters next. He dances the way he probably works — enormous, committed, unselfconscious. You remember his face from the Slide.

arrival_variants.text: You are still warm from the dissolution. The circle finds you in the warmth.
You came back to the floor. The circle was waiting.
The projection changes.

conditional_text.append: The arrow spins again. It slows. It points at you.

nodes.oor_action: The spinning arrow is not random.
nodes.subtitle: THE ARROW CIRCLE

nodes.text:
The circle is bright. The rest of the room is dark by comparison.

You can feel the aperture beneath your feet — the portal, the connection, whatever it is. It is warm and vibrating at a frequency that matches your heartbeat. Or your heartbeat has matched it. You can't tell which came first.

You dance. You are seen.

Something is taken from you that you did not know you were carrying. You feel lighter. Specifically lighter — the way you feel after crying, after confessing, after saying the thing you've been holding in the back of your throat for longer than you can account for.

The crowd absorbs you back when you finish. You are part of something. You don't know what. You don't need to.

conditional_text.append: The paint-knuckle man cheers from the ring. You can't hear what he says. The gesture is the content.

nodes.subtitle: INSIDE THE DANCE CIRCLE

nodes.text:
The arrow moves on. The circle continues without you.

Someone else steps in. The crowd watches them — the same weight of attention it aimed at you a moment ago, transferred, precise. You are standing in the ring of bodies in the space where the wanting used to be. The person in the circle is dancing and the crowd is with them and the room has moved on the way a current moves past a stone that chose to stay on the bank.

The cold begins at the back of your neck.

conditional_text.append: You've been cold before tonight. At the edge, at the bar, in the alley. It keeps finding you.

nodes.oor_action: DJ OOR does not note your refusal. DJ OOR does not note anything about you individually.
nodes.subtitle: (DELETE - DIDNT DANCE IN CIRCLE)

nodes.text:
DJ OOR has been optimizing the machine all night, running it with the expertise of an ancient technician. DJ OOR knows the time for harvest is upon us. 

The final song repeats the earlier pattern of nostalgic familiarity mixed with the frequencies of an alien context. The vocoded voice calls the crowd down. Lower. The bass descends and bodies descend with it - bending, folding, sinking. You go with them. The person next to you grabs your hand. You let them.

At the floor, face-level with the concrete, the music fades to a drone. So low you stop hearing it and start feeling it. Vibration from underneath. Vibration from above. You are between them. You are the conduit. Something passing through - not pain, not pleasure. A flow. A restoration.

Stillness. The bass drops out. Silence. Earned presence.

The portal closes. The rings flash out of existance. A change in pressure is felt by everyone, as if something has been acoustically sealed. Fog clears. Lights come up warm. The room is a warehouse.

You stand. Knees ache. Shirt damp. Smiling. The crowd disperses in the same way a slime mold dies - a collapsing collection of organized entropy fighting to give up the meta-structure and return control back to the individual cells.

arrival_variants.text: You are still light from the circle. The closing finds you light-hearted and... ready.
You watched from the ring. The closing does not care.
All good things must come to an end. It's time to complete the circle.

conditional_text.append: DJ OOR is gone. All that remains are two pink gloves draped over the mixer and a few small scraps of yellow yarn.

nodes.subtitle: THE CLOSING CEREMONY

nodes.text:
The cold outside is a relief. You breathe it in. The gravel lot is emptying. The sodium lights are still orange. The alley is the same length it was before.

You sit down on the curb. You stare up at the sky and quietly reflect on what you experienced tonight. The silence of this moment reminds you of the silence of the closing ritual. The throb of the bass still rings in your ears but it is fading fast.

You put your hand in your coat pocket and find something that wasn't there before.

arrival_variants.text: The night is over. You are standing in the cold.

conditional_text.append: All four transcendentals yielded. The Kabbalists said the world is made of light that shattered into matter — holy sparks in everything, waiting to be returned. After your experience tonight, you understand what they meant. The same process ran through the first fire circles, through every gathering where humans moved together and felt the boundary between them become optional. Tonight was one of those gatherings. What moved through you wasn't created here. It was always present — in a certain quality of winter light, kind gesture between strangers, in the moment two dancers moved to the same flow and felt it without looking. Tonight you were open and it all moved through you. What you felt was alignment: the specific resonance of focusing your presence on what is real. Goodness. Truth. Beauty. Unity. These don't require a cosmic warehouse dance party. What the experience gave you was permission — a signal loud enough to get past the reasons you keep yourself at a distance. You know the frequency now. You can find it anywhere you're willing to look for it.

A strip of fabric, thin, almost translucent. Warm. You hold it to the sodium light and there is writing on it that shifts when you try to read it. It smells like the fog from inside. Your body remembers the bass — a phantom vibration in your sternum that will take days to fully fade. You will come back.

A shard of something — glass, or crystal, or resin. It hums faintly. The sodium light refracts through it and for a moment the gravel lot looks like somewhere older. You put it back in your pocket quickly. Your body remembers the bass — a phantom vibration in your sternum that will take days to fully fade. You will come back.

A small smooth stone. Warm despite the cold. You close your hand around it and for a moment you can feel the crowd — not remember them, feel them, as if the boundary never fully reformed. Your body remembers the bass — a phantom vibration in your sternum that will take days to fully fade. You will come back.

A small knot of yarn, orange and yellow, soft. Warm. It wasn't cut — it was pulled from something. You think of the hand that held yours. You don't know their name. You know exactly how their palm felt. Your body remembers the bass — a phantom vibration in your sternum that will take days to fully fade. You will come back.

Your pockets are empty. There is nothing except the cold and the walk and the phantom vibration and the knowledge that something happened and you weren't quite in it. You will come back. You're not sure why you know this.

nodes.text:
The cold is sudden and abrasive. Not the gradual cold of the approach — the sharp cold of something warm being taken away.

The gravel lot is half-empty. The sodium lights are the same orange they were when you arrived. Your hands go back into your pockets. The bass is still audible through the walls, muffled, diminishing, and you can feel the exact moment it stops reaching your body. A border you didn't know existed until you crossed it.

You keep walking. That's enough for tonight.

arrival_variants.text: You saw enough from the edge. The antlers and the rod and the pairing — none of it made sense. You're choosing the version of tonight where it doesn't have to.
You are leaving.

conditional_text.append: The cold doesn't surprise you. You've been cold all night — at the edge, at the bar. It was looking for you.

nodes.subtitle: THE EXIT

nodes.text:
You drive home. The streets are empty. Your keys are cold. You sit on your couch and put on something — a show, a podcast, noise to fill the silence that followed you from the parking lot.

You fall asleep without deciding to. In the morning you will remember going somewhere. You will not remember where. You will not remember the bass or the warmth or the shape behind the decks. You will feel slightly wrong for a few days. You will not look for the reason.

arrival_variants.text: The building is behind you. The night is behind you. You are driving.

nodes.subtitle: THE SECURITY GUARD
nodes.text:
The guy at the door looks at you and you think you're not getting in.

He's big. SECURITY on the jacket in block letters. He's standing the way someone stands when they saw a picture of standing once and committed to it. Nothing moves except his eyes, and his eyes don't go to your face. They go lower. Your hands. Your pockets.

"Left side," he says.

You pat your left pocket. Empty. You try to remember if someone said there was a cover. He looks at you for a moment. Then something in his posture unlocks.

"You're fine.", he says as he executes a well articulated wink.

choices.text: Go through the door. Pass through the threshold.
